subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Selects and contextualizes relevant quotations to support key points.
Demonstrates satisfactory grasp of the novelâs narrative and central motifs.
Shows awareness of broader societal pressures affecting characters.
weaknesses: Interpretation remains descriptive rather than sustained or persuasive.
Lacks deeper engagement with historical context and secondary frameworks.
Occasional citation lapses (missing quotation marks, inconsistent page references).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student shows satisfactory knowledge and understanding of Evelyn Hugoâs story and selects pertinent quotations, their interpretation lacks sustained depth. References are generally relevant and supportive but not consistently exemplary. This performance aligns best with Band 3 for knowledge and understanding, acknowledging solid but not fully persuasive interpretation.

strengths: Identifies moments of vivid imagery and links them to emotional effects.
Occasional evaluative comments move beyond pure summary.
weaknesses: Analysis is often descriptive and reliant on plot retelling.
Limited exploration of authorial choices (narrative voice, structure, symbolism).
Evaluation of how textual features shape meaning is minimal and underdeveloped.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay largely summarizes events with only brief analytical insights. Authorial choices are rarely examined, and evaluation remains shallow. This aligns with Band 2, reflecting some appropriate analysis but a heavy reliance on description.

strengths: Clear overall structure with introduction, body, and conclusion.
Functional transitions that signal development of ideas.
Attempts to integrate quotations and tie them to claims.
weaknesses: Line of inquiry is only somewhat developed and occasionally meanders.
Quotation integration is uneven, with some evidence presented as stand-alone blocks.
Transitions are sometimes abrupt, and development of context-to-thesis links is inconsistent.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Organization is adequate, with a discernible line of inquiry and generally coherent progression. Evidence is sometimes integrated into the argument, matching the descriptors for Band 3: adequately organized with some development of ideas and occasional integration of supporting examples.

strengths: Language is generally clear and readable.
Vocabulary is appropriate for an HL essay.
Register is mostly academic and appropriate to the task.
weaknesses: Frequent grammatical slips (missing apostrophes, run-on sentences, comma splices).
Informal phrasings and occasional second-person usage reduce formality.
Repetitive word choices and uneven sentence lengths impede precision and flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student communicates clearly on the whole, recurring mechanical errors and lapses in academic register limit the work to Band 2: language that is sometimes clear but with noticeable inaccuracies and inconsistent style.

strandSelections.reasoning: Quotations are usually introduced or commented upon but seldom woven syntactically into the studentâs own sentences; several appear as stand-alone blocks.  Nonetheless, the essay attempts to connect evidence to claims directly after each citation, so integration is better than minimal.  This âsometimes integratedâ level matches Band 3.

The student selects relevant sections of Evelynâs childhood, early Hollywood years and her relationship with Celia, demonstrating a satisfactory grasp of the novelâs narrative and its central motif of âsexual currency.â  The commentary recognises patriarchal pressures, survival, and desperation, showing more than surfaceâlevel plot summary.  However, discussion of historical context, character complexity or Reidâs broader critique of Hollywood remains limited and undeveloped, so the interpretation is not yet sustained or persuasive.  This profile aligns with the âsatisfactory knowledge and understandingâ description of Band 3.

The essay follows a clear introduction-body-conclusion pattern and each paragraph pursues the overarching line of inquiry about âpower.â  Transitions are functional but occasionally abrupt, and reasoning within paragraphs can meander, indicating only partial cohesion.  Development of the central argument is adequate rather than thorough, situating the work in Band 3 where organization is âadequateâ and the line of inquiry âsomewhat developed.â

Quotations are supplied for every key point (pp. 43, 44, 49, 176) and are clearly tied to the argument about sex and beauty as power.  The passages chosen are pertinent, varied and generally well introduced, so they support the studentâs claims effectively.  Minor lapses (occasional missing quotation marks or page citation inconsistency) prevent the references from feeling exemplary, but they remain solidly ârelevant and supportive,â matching Band 4.

The student adopts an academic register for the most part, but rhetorical questions and occasional second-person (âweâ, âyou are tiredâ) reduce formality.  Style is therefore mostly, but not consistently, appropriate to a HL essay.  Such performance aligns with Band 3 for register and style.

Language is generally clear and readable.  Occasional grammatical slips (missing apostrophes, run-ons, comma splices) and informal phrasings (âHere is my valueâ) occur but seldom impede understanding.  Vocabulary is appropriate but not sophisticated.  This reflects âclear language with adequate accuracy,â placing the work in Band 3.

Much of the essay retells events and labels them âimmoralâ or âdesperate.â  Analytical momentsâsuch as noting Reidâs use of vivid imagery or patriarchal framingâappear but are brief and lightly developed.  Authorial choices (narrative voice, structure, symbolism) are largely unexplored, and evaluation of how these choices shape meaning is minimal.  This balance of some appropriate but mainly descriptive commentary is characteristic of Band 2.

annotations.comment: Consider refining the thesis to specify how Reidâs narrative choicesâsuch as voice or structureâunderscore sex and beauty as power, thus guiding reader expectations.
The phrase âthrough beauty and sexâ recurs; try using varied vocabulary (e.g., âseduction,â âallureâ) to enhance language precision and avoid repetition.
This paragraph provides summary and quotation but lacks analytical depth. After quoting, explicitly explain how Reidâs word choice or narrative stance shapes our understanding of Evelynâs agency.
Integrate the quotation syntactically into your sentence rather than isolating it as a block, improving cohesion and demonstrating fluent evidence integration.
The student selects a pertinent quotation that supports the argument about Evelynâs evolving self-awareness, demonstrating effective use of textual support.
Effective transition (âFurthermoreâ) signals development of the argument, and the paragraph clearly highlights the cost Evelyn incurs, linking plot to themes of survival.
Labeling the situation as âmorally wrongâ adds personal judgment but lacks examination of how Reid frames this moment through narrative perspective or tone. Consider deeper textual analysis.
The sentence starting âEvelyn needed a way outâ¦â is very lengthy. Breaking it into shorter sentences will improve readability and clarity.

The studentâs title is concise and clearly indicates the focus on Evelyn Hugoâs narrative, establishing an academic tone. Consider adding a subtitle to signal the essayâs argument for greater clarity.
The research question is clearly stated but could be more focused by specifying which aspects of humanity or survival will be examined, thereby tightening the scope of analysis.
Starting with a rhetorical question engages the reader but feels informal for an HL essay. Consider rewording to assert your thesis directly, maintaining academic register.
The introduction sets up context effectively, but the assertion of the âthird meaning of powerâ would benefit from a brief theoretical framework or secondary source to deepen knowledge.
The quotation on page 49 is introduced but cut off (`Having lost the`). Ensure full quotations and proper citation formatting to maintain academic rigor.
Describing the scene as âimmoralâ is evaluative but not analytical. Instead, discuss how Reidâs use of vivid imagery or symbolism conveys the power imbalance.
Consider exploring the significance of the contrasting ages and how Reid manipulates reader discomfort through narrative pacing or word choice.
Strong analysis of Reidâs imagery; noting how descriptive language evokes discomfort is insightful and moves beyond mere plot summary.
Phrases like âput the reader through the perspectiveâ could be tightened to âimmerse the reader in Evelynâs perspectiveâ for more precise academic style.
The observation about ambition is valid; to deepen knowledge, connect Evelynâs ambition to specific cultural expectations of women in mid-20th-century Hollywood.
The student effectively contextualizes Evelynâs bisexuality within historical constraints, showing awareness of broader societal pressures.
The connection between the historical context and the theme of sexual power could be more tightly integrated. Explicitly tie this context back to your thesis on survival strategies.
Sentence length here is very large. Breaking up the paragraph into smaller, focused chunks will improve coherence and reader engagement.
The closing statement succinctly acknowledges the themeâs culmination when Evelyn no longer needs to fight for survival, neatly tying the essayâs narrative arc.
The conclusion reiterates examples but does not discuss broader implications of Reidâs work. Consider adding reflections on universal themes or author intent.
To strengthen your conclusion, propose how the theme of sexual currency might resonate with contemporary audiences or other literary texts.
The phrase âwhat humans will do to endureâ is generic. Replace with more specific language (e.g., âthe extremities individuals traverseâ) to enhance specificity and style.
